channel.title: aws on focaaby's Note
channel.description: Recent content in aws on focaaby's Note

item.title: Automatically validating the multiple SAN through DNS validation within multiple hosted zone by using CloudFormation template
item.description: Summary ACM support automatically renews DNS-validated certificates[1]. In this article will go through how to request a public certificate with multiple Subject Alternative Names(SAN)[2], which included the domain name in a public hosted zone and a private hosted zone. Steps Create a public hosted zone, and record the hosted zone id. Create a CloudFormation template with following YAML file. --- AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Description: Test Resources: MyPrivateHostedZone: Type: AWS::Route53::HostedZone Properties: Name: dev.

item.title: Push a Helm Chart to AWS ECR
item.description: Summary AWS ECR supports pushing Open Container Initiative (OCI) artifacts[1][2] to your repositories, and this article will go through how to push a Helm chart to ECR. Also, I try to use the Helm Dependency on ECR. The ECR supported the Helm template written in Helm Dependency, but ECR repositories are still not can not add as Helm repo currently. Testing Steps Following the steps in document[2] to install the Helm client version 3.

item.title: EKS service with internal NLB, but gets timeouts randomly
item.description: Summary While deploying both client and server on their EKS cluster and connect through internal NLB, however, the communication gets timeout randomly. Problem In Kubernetes, we can expose the pod to cluster with Kubernetes Service. For sure, we can create a Kubernetes Service of type LoadBalancer, AWS NLB or CLB is provisioned that load balances network traffic. Figure source: https://www.docker.com/blog/designing-your-first-application-kubernetes-communication-services-part3/ To manage the workload easily, we might want to deploy both the client and server sides on Kubernetes.
